findings.detail: Bold label 'Human accepts without independent check' was 5 words and 'Human follows directive despite contradicting evidence' was 6 words. Revised to 'Accepts without checking' (3 words) and 'Follows despite contradicting evidence' (4 words) in cycle 1. Causal detail moved to 12px subtitle spans. Findings resolved before final write.

Clean 5-node TD branch diagram: SRC (blue) forks via 'Path A' and 'Path B' edges to PA and PB (both purple), which each flow to their respective error terminals EA and EB (both red). Two-column layout at TD direction; no node overlap or truncation expected at 1400px. All causal mechanism labels placed as 12px subtitle spans inside nodes rather than edge labels, avoiding edge-label collision risk. Color palette: blue start (source), purple path (process nodes), red error (terminal results) — semantically coherent and distinct from HITL yellow.

All five node labels trace directly to corpus §5 Core Concepts: 'Automated system produces output' (§5.1, Introduction §4), 'Accepts without checking' (§5.2 omission pathway + §5.3 cognitive offloading), 'Follows despite contradicting evidence' (§5.2 commission error definition), 'Omission error' (§5.2, §10 vocabulary), 'Commission error' (§5.2, §10 vocabulary). Subtitle terms 'Cognitive offloading', 'System 1 shortcut', 'Complacency', 'perceived reliability' are all defined in §5.3. No Judgment Framework (9.7–9.9) or other forward-topic nodes present.

Governing concept 'Automation Bias — Two Error Paths' placed in --- config title: field. No ghost node displacement of the central organizing idea. The two branch paths and their causal labels correctly represent the two error types as intermediate nodes, not title-level concepts.

Intent explicitly specifies a decision branch (two parallel paths from one source), so SRC having 2 outgoing edges is correct per the spec. This is a genuinely non-linear relationship, not a linear_flow_branched violation. The branch structure reads cleanly top-down as intended.
flowchart TD matches the spec requirement ('flowchart TD — top-down, one source node branching into two parallel paths, each ending in an error result node'). 5 nodes, 4 edges: within 3–8 node range.

preflight.mermaid_parse: pass (valid --- config block with title/theme/themeVariables/flowchart keys; flowchart TD declaration; bracket-notation nodes with HTML labels; classDef and class assignments all resolved; edge labels quoted)
preflight.node_count: pass (5 nodes: SRC, PA, PB, EA, EB — within 3–8 range)
preflight.label_discipline: pass (all bold labels ≤ 4 words after cycle-1 revision: 'Automated system produces output' 4w, 'Accepts without checking' 3w, 'Follows despite contradicting evidence' 4w, 'Omission error' 2w, 'Commission error' 2w)
preflight.concept_fidelity: pass (all labels trace to corpus §5.1–5.3 Core Concepts; subtitles trace to §5.3 mechanisms; no adjacent-topic drift)
preflight.jargon_labels: pass (no bare unexplained acronyms; 'System 1' appears only in a subtitle, defined in corpus §5.3 via prerequisite 9.1; all bold node labels are plain English)
preflight.title_discipline: pass (governing concept in config title: field; no ghost nodes used)
preflight.branch_discipline: pass (intent calls for a two-path decision branch; SRC outgoing edges are intentional non-linear structure, not a collapsed-linear violation)
